security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner detected 'SAM' as Windows SAM database (actually Segment Anything Model), backticks as shell execution (actually skill name formatting), and misidentified cryptographic patterns. This is a legitimate computer vision expert skill with no security concerns.

low_findings.title: External Commands Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at line 72, but this is simply skill names in backticks (`ai-engineer`, `robotics-expert`). No actual shell execution present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 72 contains only comma-separated skill names in backticks, no code execution. Pattern match is a false positive from regex.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM Database Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Windows SAM database' at multiple lines, but 'SAM' here refers to Segment Anything Model (SAM 3), a Meta computer vision model, not the Windows Security Account Manager.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All references to 'SAM' in this document clearly refer to 'SAM 3' (Segment Anything Model 3), a computer vision segmentation model, not the Windows security database.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at several lines, but there is no cryptographic functionality in this skill. Likely a pattern matching artifact.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code or algorithms present in this document. This appears to be a false positive triggered by keyword matching.

content.user_title: Build Computer Vision Systems with SOTA Models
content.value_statement: Building modern computer vision systems requires expertise in multiple SOTA models. This skill provides expert guidance on YOLO26, SAM 3, and Vision Language Models for detection, segmentation, and spatial analysis tasks.
actual_capabilities: Design real-time object detection systems using YOLO26 NMS-free architecture
Implement text-to-mask segmentation using SAM 3 with natural language prompts
Build Vision Language Model pipelines for visual question answering and grounding
Optimize vision models for edge deployment using ONNX and TensorRT
Create depth estimation and 3D reconstruction systems using monocular cameras
Integrate classical geometry with deep learning for calibration and SLAM
limitations: SAM 3 requires significant VRAM; quantized versions needed for local GPU inference
Text-guided segmentation may produce ambiguous results with vague prompts
Real-time performance depends on hardware; edge devices may need model distillation

use_cases.title: Industrial Quality Inspection
use_cases.description: Build automated visual inspection systems using YOLO26 for fast defect detection and SAM 3 for precise segmentation of product anomalies on manufacturing lines.

use_cases.title: Autonomous Robot Navigation
use_cases.description: Create vision pipelines for mobile robots using depth estimation, visual SLAM, and real-time object detection for safe navigation in dynamic environments.
use_cases.target_user: Robotics developers and autonomous systems engineers

use_cases.title: Document and Media Analysis
use_cases.description: Implement VLM-powered systems for extracting structured data from images, diagrams, and visual documents through visual grounding and question answering.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Object Detection Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up YOLO26 for real-time object detection on my edge device? I need to detect vehicles and pedestrians.

prompt_templates.title: Text-Guided Segmentation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to segment all instances of 'the red shipping container' in this image using SAM 3. How do I use the text-to-mask feature?

prompt_templates.title: VLM Visual Question Answering
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build a pipeline using Florence-2 to answer questions about images. I want to extract structured data from product photos.

prompt_templates.title: Edge Optimization Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: What's the best approach to optimize a YOLO26 model for deployment on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin? I need to maintain accuracy while reducing latency.

output_examples.input: How do I set up YOLO26 for real-time object detection on my edge device?
output_examples.output:
For YOLO26 edge deployment, use the NMS-free architecture which simplifies export. Key steps:

1. **Model Selection**: Use yolo26n for nano (fastest) or yolo26s for small (better accuracy)
2. **Export Command**: `yolo export model=yolo26s format=onnx`
3. **TensorRT Optimization**: Convert to TensorRT FP16 for 2x speedup on Jetson
4. **Inference**: Use the simplified inference API without NMS post-processing

The NMS-free design reduces latency by 30% compared to traditional YOLO architectures.

output_examples.input: Using SAM 3 for text-guided segmentation of specific objects
output_examples.output:
SAM 3's text-to-mask capability allows segmentation using natural language:

```python
from sam3 import SAM3Model

model = SAM3Model()
image = load_image('warehouse.jpg')
# Text prompt for segmentation
masks = model.text_to_mask(image, prompt='the blue container')
```

This eliminates the need for manual point prompts. SAM 3 achieves 2x accuracy over SAM 2 by unifying detection, segmentation, and tracking in one model.

best_practices: Use NMS-free architectures like YOLO26 for lower latency in real-time applications
Combine YOLO26 for fast candidate proposal with SAM 3 for precise mask refinement
Use descriptive text prompts with SAM 3 (e.g., 'the 5mm bolt' instead of 'bolt')
anti_patterns: Using manual NMS post-processing instead of NMS-free architectures
Relying on click-only segmentation instead of text-guided prompts with SAM 3
Using legacy export pipelines that do not support YOLO26 simplified module structure

faq.question: What is YOLO26 and how does it differ from previous YOLO versions?
faq.answer: YOLO26 is the latest YOLO architecture featuring an NMS-free design that eliminates Non-Maximum Suppression post-processing. This reduces latency and complexity while maintaining high accuracy. It also includes improved small object recognition through ProgLoss and STAL assignment.

faq.question: How does SAM 3 text-to-mask work?
faq.answer: SAM 3 can segment objects using natural language descriptions instead of manual point prompts. You provide a text prompt like 'the blue container on the right' and SAM 3 generates masks for matching objects using its built-in visual-language alignment.

faq.question: Can I run SAM 3 on consumer hardware?
faq.answer: SAM 3 requires significant VRAM. For local GPU inference, use quantized or distilled versions of the model. Consider SAM 3 Mobile or SAM 3 Tiny for edge devices with limited memory.

faq.question: What are Vision Language Models and when should I use them?
faq.answer: VLMs like Florence-2, PaliGemma 2, and Qwen2-VL combine visual understanding with language reasoning. Use them when you need to answer questions about images, extract structured data, or perform visual grounding tasks.

faq.question: How do I optimize YOLO26 for edge deployment?
faq.answer: Export YOLO26 to ONNX format for broad compatibility, then convert to TensorRT for NVIDIA devices. Use FP16 precision for 2x speedup. The NMS-free architecture simplifies the export process compared to older YOLO versions.

faq.question: What is the relationship between this skill and Claude Code?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude Code, Codex, and Claude to help you design, implement, and optimize computer vision pipelines. The AI assistant uses this expertise to guide you through vision system development tasks.
